vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are either absent, dormant, or obscured by debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (Yellow Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; appears to be bare soil/substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter; appears to be bare soil/substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: The 'White Rabbit' anchor is present, but the Pothos is not visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P4 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine present; no foliage detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshells or perlite) in P3. A metallic object (spoon) appears in T-3 and is removed by T-1.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Introduction of a metallic spoon into the biome.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Removal of the metallic spoon; lighting shift.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Introduction of a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or marker) near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Cylindrical object remains; no biological change.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Biological Stasis' or 'Systemic Failure'. There is no evidence of active photosynthesis or vegetative growth. The environment appears to be maintained as a substrate-only setup or has suffered total specimen loss.
anomalies: Presence of a white cylindrical sensor/marker (T-1 to CURRENT).
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a desk-based biome that is currently devoid of visible plant life. While the pots and the scale anchor (white rabbit) are present, the expected botanical specimens are not. The environment has been subject to minor human interventions, such as the temporary placement of a spoon and a cylindrical sensor, but these have not stimulated any visible plant growth. The soil remains dark and damp, suggesting regular moisture, yet the lack of foliage indicates a critical failure in the biological health of the system.
